column2: if profile: ctx.inject(f"User context: {profile}") # Honcho backend implementation class HonchoBackend(UserModelingBackend): base_url: str = "https://api.honcho.dev" api_key: str async def get_profile(self, user_id: str) -> UserProfile: async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client: resp = await client.get(f"{self.base_url}/users/{user_id}/profile") return UserProfile(**resp.json()) async def ask(self, user_id: str, question: str) -> str: """Dialectic Q&A — synthesizes answer from interaction history.""" async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client: resp = await client.post(f"{self.base_url}/users/{user_id}/context", json={"question": question}) return resp.json()["answer"] Who has this Framework User modeling? Details Yes — via Hermes Profile, semantic search, dialectic Q&A, prefetch Honcho Google ADK Partial Scoped state per user, but no external modeling Memory types include "user memory" but no external Mastra Partial service Claude Partial Auto-memory for user preferences, but local only Code Others Generally no Most have flat key-value memory Depends on Memory capability #30 — Memory capability (complementary, not dependent, but should compose well) Speculative Prefetch for Capabilities (async background data loading) #107 — Speculative Prefetch (for zero-latency profile injection) Related Observational Memory capability (observe/reflect with token thresholds) #68 — Observational Memory (auto-observe user patterns — could feed into user modeling)
